question: Describe the primary responsibilities of an operating system in resource management.
expectedPoints: Managing and allocating CPU time to processes via scheduling algorithms.
Controlling and coordinating the use of memory among various applications.
Managing access to input/output devices to prevent conflicts and ensure efficient use.

question: Explain the main difference between a monolithic kernel and a microkernel architecture.
expectedPoints: A monolithic kernel runs all operating system services (like device drivers, file system, scheduler) in the same kernel space.
A microkernel minimizes what runs in kernel space, moving most services (servers) to user space.
The key difference is the size and scope of the code running in the privileged kernel mode, impacting stability and security.

question: Identify two functions of the OS that are part of resource management.
expectedPoints: Process Management: Creating, scheduling, and terminating processes.
Memory Management: Allocating and deallocating memory space to programs.
